title: Italy Electronic Identity Card Request (Carta d'Identità Elettronica, CIE)
description: "Domanda per il rilascio della Carta d'Identità Elettronica" (Request for issuance of the Electronic Identity Card), the intake form an adult Italian resident presents in person to the Ufficio Anagrafe (civil-registry office) of their Comune (municipality) of residence or domicile, under art.2 commi 9-10 of Legge 15 maggio 1997 n.127 and art.76 D.P.R. 445/2000 (declarations under penalty of law). The CIE itself is a nationally standardized, Ministero dell'Interno-operated document (see cartaidentita.interno.gov.it) produced identically by the Istituto Poligrafico e Zecca dello Stato regardless of which of Italy's roughly 8,000 comuni receives the request; this schema is sourced from one concrete municipal exemplar — Comune di Tre Ville (provincia di Trento) — following this registry's established de/bmi cross-jurisdiction-exemplar convention for nationally-set but locally-administered processes. This document models the adult (maggiorenne) request only: applicant identity/birth/residence data, the three-way request-type declaration (first issuance / renewal for expiry / renewal for theft-loss-deterioration), the two-way expatriation-validity declaration, height, the mandatory organ-and-tissue-donation declaration (consent / refusal / no preference expressed — a well-documented national feature of the CIE issuance process, corroborated independently below), the delivery/pickup-method choice (direct postal delivery to the applicant vs. a named delegate) together with a choice of the issuing comune's own named collection desks, and the two applicant-supplied attachments (a passport-style photograph, and — only for the theft/loss/deterioration renewal option — a police loss/theft report). It deliberately does NOT model: the office-only processing strip at the foot of the form (a fee-paid checkbox and an old/expired-document-surrendered tracking checkbox, both ticked by the municipal clerk after the counter transaction, not supplied by the applicant); the wet-ink signature line; the separate minor's (per i minori) variant of this same form, which replaces the organ-donation declaration with a parent/guardian declarant and a minor's-identity block, and which — when the CIE is requested valid for expatriation — requires a distinct "Atto di assenso all'espatrio" (deed of consent to expatriation) signed by the other parent or holder of parental responsibility (or a giudice tutelare authorization); or any of the in-person biometric steps (fingerprint capture, live photo, registry-data verification) that the national portal describes as happening at the counter after this form is presented, which are procedural/office-side and not applicant-form data. This overturns a prior dead-end finding: this registry's own it/agenzia-entrate/modello-730 VERIFICATION.md (GOV-2382) recorded Italy's National ID vertical as "fully appointment-based... no downloadable form" — that screening pass missed this municipal-form-publishing pattern, present across many of Italy's comuni, of which Comune di Tre Ville is the concrete exemplar used here. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Government of Italy, the Ministero dell'Interno, or any Comune.

authority.name: Comune — Ufficio Anagrafe (the applicant's municipality of residence or domicile; concrete exemplar sourced: Comune di Tre Ville, provincia di Trento)

operatedBy.name: Ministero dell'Interno
operatedBy.basis: Decreto-Legge 25 giugno 2008, n. 112 (convertito con Legge 6 agosto 2008, n. 133), che ha introdotto la Carta d'Identità Elettronica; art.2 commi 9-10 Legge 15 maggio 1997, n. 127; art.76 D.P.R. 28 dicembre 2000, n. 445 (dichiarazioni sotto responsabilità penale); art.3 Legge 21 novembre 1967, n. 1185 (condizioni di validità per l'espatrio). The Comune (municipality), through its Ufficio Anagrafe, is the receiving/issuing authority for civil-registry purposes (art.54 D.Lgs. 267/2000; art.3 T.U.L.P.S.), while the Ministero dell'Interno designs, standardizes, and operates the national CIE system, its production pipeline, and the cartaidentita.interno.gov.it portal.

source.documentRef: Domanda per il rilascio della Carta d'Identità Elettronica (adult/maggiorenne request), Comune di Tre Ville, published at https://www.comune.treville.tn.it/Amministrazione/Documenti-e-dati/Modulistica/Modulo-richiesta-carta-d-identita-elettronica-CIE

verification.notes: GOV-3050, a pre-scouted GOV-3045/GOV-3054 child issue authoring Italy's National ID vertical opener — the last of Italy's six verticals (DMV, Taxes, Visa, Passport, Business Formation were already covered; Italy reaches 6/6 with this schema). Overturns this registry's own GOV-2382 'confirmed dead end' finding for Italy's National ID vertical, which rested on a screening pass that only checked the fully appointment-gated national CIE booking portal (prenotazionicie.interno.gov.it) and never discovered this municipal-form-publishing pattern. Independently re-fetched this cycle via direct curl (not WebFetch, which hard-fails on some of this comune's own onward links): all three PDFs named in the issue returned HTTP 200, content-type application/pdf, with byte sizes and sha256 hashes matching the issue's own citations exactly — adult request 233,313 bytes / sha256 2512f791c0a4a47b55158f91dbb379be609b99943531c8657416d98161b13e57; minor request 232,186 bytes / sha256 2204cc92bef81ef92e13f819e0f17bc47ce5d1994f076dfc93325c7f9284eb74; parental-consent (ASSENSO) deed 517,087 bytes / sha256 d877c51a581468041f312f3a940c38409def6c979bafaeec0a22ce92c64546fc (the issue's own body cites this hash correctly in full; only the abbreviated version embedded in this task's own prompt text was truncated/mis-pasted, not a real mismatch). Extracted via pdfjs-dist 4.10.38 (legacy/build/pdf.mjs): all three PDFs are genuinely blank, non-AcroForm templates — page.getAnnotations() returns zero Widget annotations on every page of all three files — with underscore-rule fill-ins on a real vector-text layer (149/136/70 text items on each form's page 1 respectively, extracted cleanly via getTextContent() with y-coordinate row-clustering, no OCR or image-XObject rendering needed). This schema models the adult request only (791db8cc...pdf), following this registry's de/bmi/national-identity-card-application precedent for a nationally-standardized-but-locally-administered process: the folder is named for the national ministry that designs/operates the CIE system (ministero-dell-interno), while authority.name records the genuinely local front-line office (a generic 'Comune — Ufficio Anagrafe', with the concrete Tre Ville exemplar disclosed). The minor request and the ASSENSO consent deed were fetched and read in full as corroborating evidence that this is a genuine, consistent national form family (not a one-off municipal invention) and are deliberately not modelled here, since they cover a materially different declarant/consent chain (a parent or guardian declaring on behalf of a minor, with no organ-donation section at all, and an additional cross-form consent requirement) — left as a natural follow-up companion schema, mirroring this registry's own it/poliziadistato precedent of authoring only the maggiorenni (adult) passport request and leaving the minori variant for later. Cross-checked against the national portal (cartaidentita.interno.gov.it, requires the www. prefix or DNS fails to resolve — confirmed again this cycle): 'request-cie' (HTTP 200) confirms the CIE 'can be requested at any Municipality' and that a dedicated minors' page exists separately; 'release-and-renew-in-italy' (HTTP 200 after a same-host redirect) states the fee ('a fee of €16.79 plus the fixed and secretarial fees, where applicable') — consistent with the source form's own printed total, 'PAGATO € 22,20' (an office-ticked total inclusive of the fixed/secretarial components, not a discrepancy) — states fingerprints and a photograph are captured/provided at the counter, states the applicant 'Indicates the preferred modality of collection of the document (delivery to a specified address or withdrawal from the Town Hall)' (independently corroborating that this schema's deliveryMethod/pickupOffice fields are genuinely applicant-facing, not office-reserved), states the applicant 'Provides the consent or denial to organ donation' (independently corroborating organDonationDeclaration as a real, national — not Tre-Ville-specific — feature of this process), and states that a CIE parent applicant must present the other parent's/guardian's consent-to-expatriation deed (independently corroborating the ASSENSO PDF's real-world role, reinforcing the decision to leave the minor path out of this schema's scope rather than conflate it in). Field count: 15 fields[] entries, 2 documents[] entries — directly counted from this schema.json, not estimated.

fields.name: applicantFullName
fields.label: Il Sottoscritto
fields.description: Full name of the undersigned adult applicant, declared under the criminal-liability provisions of art.76 D.P.R. 445/2000 cited in the form's own header.

fields.name: requestType
fields.label: Chiede il rilascio della Carta d'Identità Elettronica
fields.description: The reason for the CIE request: a first issuance, a renewal because the previous card expired, or a renewal because the previous card was stolen, lost, or damaged. The source prints these three options as a single-select checkbox row; documents[].policeLossReportCopy is required only for the third option.
fields.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'CHIEDE IL RILASCIO DELLA CARTA D'IDENTITÀ ELETTRONICA — prima emissione / Rinnovo per scadenza / Rinnovo per furto/smarrimento/deterioramento'

fields.name: expatriationValidity
fields.label: Valida per l'espatrio / Non valida per l'espatrio
fields.description: Whether the applicant requests the card be valid for foreign travel (espatrio) or not. Per art.3 Legge 1185/1967, a card valid for expatriation cannot be issued to an applicant in one of the conditions obstructing passport/expatriation-document issuance.
fields.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'valida per l'espatrio / non valida per l'espatrio'

fields.name: birthPlace
fields.label: Di essere nato/a a
fields.description: Municipality (or foreign place) of the applicant's birth.
fields.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'Di essere nato/a a ___'

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: il (data di nascita)
fields.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'Di essere nato/a a ___ il ___'

fields.name: residenceStreetAddress
fields.label: Di essere residente in [Comune], Via
fields.description: Street or square name of the applicant's residence address within the filing Comune. The Comune name itself is pre-printed on the source form (the exemplar reads 'Di essere residente in Tre Ville, Via ___') rather than filled in by the applicant, since the request is by definition filed at the Comune of residence or domicile.
fields.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'Di essere residente in Tre Ville, Via ___'

fields.name: residenceHouseNumber
fields.label: nr. (numero civico)
fields.description: House/street number of the applicant's residence address.

fields.name: heightCm
fields.label: statura in cm
fields.description: The applicant's height in centimetres, printed on the issued card, declared under 'Di avere i seguenti contrassegni' (identifying marks).
fields.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'Di avere i seguenti contrassegni: - statura cm. ___'

fields.name: organDonationDeclaration
fields.label: Donazione organi e tessuti
fields.description: The applicant's declaration on organ and tissue donation after death for transplant purposes: consent, refusal, or no preference expressed. Collecting this declaration at CIE issuance is a well-documented national feature of the Italian process (independently corroborated against cartaidentita.interno.gov.it's own 'Release and renew in Italy' page, which lists 'Provides the consent or denial to organ donation' among the in-person steps), not a Tre-Ville-specific addition.
fields.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'PER QUANTO RIGUARDA LA DONAZIONE ORGANI E TESSUTI DICHIARA: di acconsentire / di non acconsentire / di non esprimere alcuna volontà'

fields.name: applicationDate
fields.label: Tre Ville, lì
fields.description: Date of the applicant's declaration/signature, printed on the same line as the place of signing (the filing Comune) and immediately above the wet-ink signature line (not itself modelled, per this registry's established convention for bare signature lines).
fields.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'Tre Ville, lì ___ Firmato'

fields.name: deliveryMethod
fields.label: Spedizione diretta all'utente / Delega a
fields.description: How the applicant chooses to receive the issued card: direct postal delivery to the applicant's own address, or collection by a named delegate. Independently corroborated as a genuine applicant-facing choice (not office-reserved) by cartaidentita.interno.gov.it's own 'Release and renew in Italy' page, which lists 'Indicates the preferred modality of collection of the document (delivery to a specified address or withdrawal from the Town Hall)' among the applicant's own in-person steps.
fields.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'SPEDIZIONE DIRETTA ALL'UTENTE / DELEGA A ___'

fields.name: delegateFullName
fields.label: Delega a
fields.description: Full name of the person the applicant delegates to collect the issued card, required only when deliveryMethod is 'delega'.

fields.name: pickupOffice
fields.label: Ritiro
fields.description: Which of the issuing Comune's own named service desks the card is to be made available at for collection. The three options are specific to Comune di Tre Ville's own office network (an amalgamated municipality with multiple local desks); a different comune's own form would list its own local desks in their place, but every comune's form carries an equivalent office-selection choice.
fields.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'RITIRO: SEDE DI RAGOLI / Sportello Montagne / Sportello M. di Campiglio'

documents.label: 1 fotografia (formato fototessera) recente e a capo scoperto, riproducente le proprie sembianze
documents.handling: One recent, bare-headed, passport-style (fototessera) photograph, per the form's own 'ALLA PRESENTE ALLEGA' (attached hereto) list.
documents.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'ALLA PRESENTE ALLEGA: 1 fotografia...'

documents.label: Dichiarazione della locale Autorità di P.S. comprovante la denuncia di smarrimento
documents.handling: A declaration from the local Public Security Authority (Polizia di Stato / Carabinieri) evidencing the loss/theft report, per the form's own 'ove ricorra il caso' (where applicable) qualifier — the source form combines theft, loss, and mere deterioration into a single requestType option without a separate sub-field, so this requiredWhen condition is gated on the whole option as printed, a disclosed judgment call rather than a fabricated finer-grained trigger not present on the source.
documents.sourceRef: Domanda CIE p.1/2, 'ALLA PRESENTE ALLEGA: ... Dichiarazione della locale Autorità di P.S. comprovante la denuncia di smarrimento (ove ricorra il caso)'
